description_long: This helicopter features a streamlined, military-style fuselage with a dark green and light gray camouflage paint scheme. The main rotor is long and slender, with four blades extending from a central hub, while the tail boom is elongated and ends in a small tail rotor. The cockpit is enclosed with large, angular windows that provide wide visibility for the crew, and the nose is equipped with sensors or weapon mounts. The landing skids are robust, tubular, and painted in a matching color, providing stable ground support. Additional details include side-mounted equipment, antennas, and subtle orange markings on the rotor tips and tail.
description: A military-style helicopter with a green and gray camouflage body, four-blade main rotor, angular cockpit windows, and sturdy landing skids.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow nose, angular cockpit windows, and the symmetrical spread of the landing skids.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the elongated fuselage, four-blade main rotor, and the full length of the landing skids and tail boom.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail boom, tail rotor, and the symmetry of the main rotor blades.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile, similar to view 1, with visible cockpit, rotor, and landing skids.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored black with orange tips, and extending symmetrically from the central hub.
attribute_tail_rotor: Present at the end of the tail boom, with two blades oriented vertically, colored black with a yellow tip, and partially protected by the tail structure.
attribute_landing_skids: Two tubular landing skids, painted in green and gray, with crossbars for structural support and footpads at the ends for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple large, angular cockpit windows wrapping around the front and sides, slightly tinted, with black framing and no visible markings.
